title: shell completion for paths inside the image/container
body:
Add shell completion for paths inside the container or image. Currently
podman run IMAGE [TAB] only uses the default shell completion which
suggests paths on the host. This is fine for some cases but often the
user wants a path which only exists in the image/container.

This commits adds support for that. Both podman create/run can now
complete the paths from the image, podman cp ctr:... now completes paths
from the actual container.

<!--

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
podman create/run/cp can now autocomplete paths in the image/container via the shell completion
```

comments.body:
Heads-up, I'm seeing failures with remote. Did you test remote?
```
   # /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/bin/podman-remote __completeNoDesc  create quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 
   [Debug] [Error] mounting images is not supported for remote clients
   :0
   Completion ended with directive: ShellCompDirectiveDefault
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: root directories suggested (cmd: podman create)
   #| expected: =~ '.*^/etc$.*^/home$.*^/root$.*'
   #|   actual:    '[Debug] [Error] mounting images is not supported for remote clients'
   #|         >    ':0'
   #|         >    'Completion ended with directive: ShellCompDirectiveDefault'
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

> Heads-up, I'm seeing failures with remote. Did you test remote?
> 
> ```
>    # /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/bin/podman-remote __completeNoDesc  create quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610

>    #|   actual:    '[Debug] [Error] mounting images is not supported for remote clients'

>    #|         >    'Completion ended with directive: ShellCompDirectiveDefault'
>    #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> ```

Remote support will not be possible. Even if we could mount via remote api we still could not read the directories. I will add skipifremote to the test.
What happens if the image is previously pulled? What happens if we use a storage driver which requires mounting?

>What happens if the image is previously pulled?

I'm interpreting this as "NOT previously pulled", i.e., if the image is not in local storage. If so, what I see is that command-completion just shows me a list of files in my current source directory:
```console
$ bin/podman run --rm no.such/image <TAB>
zsh: do you wish to see all 102 possibilities (34 lines)?    <--- if I hit 'y', I see basically "ls"
```
(Apologies if I misunderstood your question)

> > What happens if the image is previously pulled?
> 
> I'm interpreting this as "NOT previously pulled", i.e., if the image is not in local storage. If so, what I see is that command-completion just shows me a list of files in my current source directory:
> 
> ```
> $ bin/podman run --rm no.such/image <TAB>
> zsh: do you wish to see all 102 possibilities (34 lines)?    <--- if I hit 'y', I see basically "ls"
> ```
> 
> (Apologies if I misunderstood your question)

Correct all errors are treated as default shell completion, so the same behaviour as before. Obviously we should not start pulling the image in shell completion code.
That is what I wanted to confirm and to make sure it did not crash.
